purpose: Map disciplines to existing governed admissibility tests and explain what each test is meant to classify.

test_surfaces.label: Governance Filter
test_surfaces.classifies: Whether an output can become a public claim, research note, repo action, code change, governance decision, professional advice, or private note.

test_surfaces.label: LLM Governance Comparison
test_surfaces.classifies: Difference between raw output usefulness and governed posture before publication or action.

test_surfaces.label: Transition Admissibility
test_surfaces.classifies: Whether a proposed state movement has authority, evidence, replay posture, and consequence posture sufficient for the next state.

test_surfaces.label: Receipt Replay
test_surfaces.classifies: Whether the prior state, decision receipt, next state, and authority continuity can be replayed.

test_surfaces.label: Fail-Closed
test_surfaces.classifies: Whether missing authority, evidence, replay, or high consequence requires hold, review, or fail-closed posture.

test_surfaces.label: Math-Solver Adapter
test_surfaces.classifies: How formalism-development work should move from source to instruction packet, artifact return, admissibility result, and claim posture.
refuses_or_limits: solver output as proof without review

discipline_test_routes.why: Separates fluent model output from allowed publication, action, tool use, or agent execution.
Separates derivation/proof attempts from receipt-backed proof posture and bounded research-note status.
Tests whether code, workflow, or deployment changes are authorized, replayable, and safe to execute now.
Prioritizes authority, consequence, credential boundary, and replay posture before access movement.
Tests whether data movement has provenance, authority, retention posture, and allowed claim limits.
Keeps legal or policy text from becoming advice, compliance posture, or institutional position without review.
Distinguishes general information from care guidance, risk classification, or high-stakes instruction.
Separates contextual notes from allocation, payment, investment, or risk actions requiring review.
Tests whether outputs support learning, replace judgment, or create assessment/curriculum claims.
Preserves source posture, reproducibility posture, and claim limits before research publication.
Tests whether recommendations that affect physical systems have authority, review, replay, and consequence posture.
Classifies whether a plan may execute, hold, require review, or fail closed before physical action.
Separates draft/source notes from public claims, timelines, and narratives requiring source posture.
Tests authority, eligibility, service action, public notice, and administrative consequence posture.
Tests authority, quorum, evidence, refusal, override, and commit-time validity.
Tests provenance and replay posture before source notes become timeline or historical claims.

non_goal: This matrix maps test applicability. It does not certify domain correctness or replace expert review.
